2606.16047.title: From Argument Components to Graphs: A Multi-Agent Debate with Confidence Gating for Argument Relations
2606.16047.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly assessed and utilized in the field of Argument Mining (AM), thanks to their strong general reasoning capabilities. However, standard training-free models often miss sophisticated details, specifically in contexts where two parts of the text have to be analyzed together. Furthermore, self-correction mechanisms tend to reinforce initial hallucinations in reasoning. Overcoming these limitations typically requires expensive, domain-specific supervised fine-tuning. Recent work has shown that a multi-agent paradigm can address such weaknesses for the component classification task through dialectical refinement with a Proponent-Opponent-Judge architecture, setting a promising direction for training-free approaches in the field. In this paper, we extend and evaluate this framework on the Argument Relation Identification and Classification (ARIC) task, reformulating it as a debate over component pairs. Besides that, we introduce a confidence gating mechanism that enables debating only on the uncertain cases and accepting the initial prediction when confidence is high. On the UKP Argument Annotated Essays v2 corpus, we demonstrate that the selective debate achieves the highest Macro F1 among all training-free methods, while debate over all samples degrades performance below that of one of the baselines. All generative approaches also outperform fine-tuned RoBERTa models on Macro F1, suggesting that the under-representation of the Attack class was more damaging to supervised fine-tuning than to inference-only models. Additionally, our framework produces human-readable debate transcripts, offering interpretability absent from both single-agent and supervised classifiers.
2606.16047.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在論證挖掘（AM）領域中越來越受到評估和利用，這要歸功於它們強大的推理能力。<lb/>然而，標準的無需訓練模型往往會錯過複雜的細節，特別是在需要同時分析文本的兩個部分的上下文中。<lb/>此外，自我修正機制往往會加強推理中的初始錯誤。<lb/>克服這些限制通常需要昂貴的特定領域監督微調。<lb/>最近的研究顯示，多代理範式可以通過提倡者-反對者-裁判架構進行辯證精煉，來解決組件分類任務中的這些弱點，為該領域的無需訓練方法設定了一個有前景的方向。<lb/>在本文中，我們擴展並評估了這一框架在論證關係識別與分類（ARIC）任務上的應用，將其重新表述為對組件對的辯論。<lb/>此外，我們引入了一種信心閘機制，使得僅在不確定的案例上進行辯論，並在信心高時接受初步預測。<lb/>在UKP論證註釋論文v2語料庫上，我們展示了選擇性辯論在所有無需訓練的方法中實現了最高的宏觀F1，而對所有樣本的辯論則使性能降至低於某些基準的水平。<lb/>所有生成方法在宏觀F1上也超越了微調的RoBERTa模型，這表明攻擊類的不足代表性對監督微調的影響比對僅推理模型更為嚴重。<lb/>此外，我們的框架生成了可讀的辯論記錄，提供了單代理和監督分類器所缺乏的可解釋性。
2606.16047.author: Jakub Bąba et.al.
